summary: Added a repo-supported Linux direct capture build preflight that proves nix develop exposes the Wayland/GBM/libdrm build surface needed to rebuild or check the M2 direct capture helper path, while documenting the exact non-product boundary.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence records the M2 audit residual risk and the exact direct-capture helper reproducibility boundary.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-direct-capture-helper-reproducibility/notes.md and docs/DIRECT_CAPTURE_HELPER_PREFLIGHT.md record the temporary-nix-shell residual risk and the build/preflight-only boundary.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The Linux dev environment exposes or documents the required Wayland/GBM build tools for the helper path.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: nix/flake.nix adds pkg-config, wayland-scanner, wayland, wayland-protocols, libgbm, and libdrm; evidence/m4-direct-capture-helper-reproducibility/commands.log records versions from nix develop.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A bounded build/preflight command proves the helper path is reproducible, or a precise blocker/follow-up is recorded.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight` passed and compiled/linked generated Wayland protocol code with Wayland client, GBM, and libdrm.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product capture, portal, capture-to-NVENC, zero-copy, cross-device, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: docs/DIRECT_CAPTURE_HELPER_PREFLIGHT.md and evidence/m4-direct-capture-helper-reproducibility/notes.md document the build-only boundary and exclusions.
Orchestrator rerun passed after evidence/report edits.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: `nix flake check` passes after adding Linux dev-shell packages.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node is a repo-quality build/preflight reproducibility fix. Runtime capture and hardware behavior remain out of scope.
